title: Minu knows financial, employee wellness are connected, so it built 30 gamified benefits
dek: Two years after profiling minu, the company has over 300 enterprise customers and its revenue grew more than five times between 2021 and 2022.

title: Amplifica Capital ‘wants to be the fund that LatAm’s female tech founders reach out to first’
dek: Amplifica invests mainly in teams where there are female founders or co-founders or goods and services focused on women.

title: Early-stage Mexico fintech Aviva is making loans as easy as a video call
dek: Aviva uses AI and natural language processing to match customers’ spoken words with fields of a loan application. Within minutes, customers can qualify for up to $1,000.

title: Perfekto bags $1.1M to find homes for imperfect produce in Mexico
dek: Perfekto's customers can personalize their subscription box of imperfect produce, of which the company says 1 million pounds have been rescued from landfills since 2021.

title: Mattilda wants to take over payment collection for Latin America’s private schools
dek: The company's SaaS tool enables parents to make private school tuition payments from a personalized payment link sent to them via email or WhatsApp.

title: Kaszek, YC back DolarApp’s mission to ‘dollarize’ Latin America’s finances with crypto
dek: With DolarApp, users can open a bank account going from peso to dollar dominated stablecoin USD Coin (USDc) and back in seconds.

title: 3 investors explain why earned wage access startups are set to cash more checks
dek: It always feels good to get paid, so it’s no surprise that a payroll model like earned wage access (EWA), which lets employees withdraw their accrued wages at any time, has exploded in popularity.

title: For LatAm payment orchestration startups, market fragmentation is a blessing in disguise
dek: To be successful in Latin America, payment orchestrators must provide a robust value proposition that meets the unique conditions of the region.
